title: Feature Engineering
text:
q.feat collects feature engineering tools under one namespace, organized as submodules:
All indicator functions share the same convention: they accept an OHLCV DataFrame with lowercase columns (open, high, low, close, volume) — the frame layout returned by q.vendors — and preserve the datetime index on the output. Indicators that only need a price series also accept a plain Series (treated as close).

title: Feature Engineering
text:
qta — our own features
q.feat.qta.sma(ohlc["close"], 20)
q.feat.qta.lags(ohlc["close"], [1, 5, 21])
To add a feature, create qrt/feat/qta/<feature>.py with one public function and re-export it in qrt/feat/qta/__init__.py (one import line + one __all__ entry).

title: Feature Engineering
text:
TA-Lib
q.feat.talib.RSI(ohlc)                    # Series named 'rsi'
q.feat.talib.ATR(ohlc, timeperiod=20)     # uses high/low/close
q.feat.talib.MACD(ohlc)                   # DataFrame: macd, macdsignal, macdhist
q.feat.talib.SMA(prices, timeperiod=20)   # Series in, Series out
Single-output indicators return a Series named after the indicator; multi-output indicators return a DataFrame with TA-Lib’s output names as columns.

title: Feature Engineering
text:
pandas-ta-classic
q.feat.pandas_ta.rsi(ohlc)                  # Series 'RSI_14'
q.feat.pandas_ta.bbands(ohlc, length=20)    # DataFrame BBL/BBM/BBU...
q.feat.pandas_ta.cdl_pattern(ohlc, name="doji")
Outputs keep pandas-ta’s parameterised names (e.g. RSI_14, MACD_12_26_9).

title: Feature Engineering
text:
IDE support
The talib and pandas_ta wrappers create their functions dynamically at runtime. Autocomplete and hover docs are provided by generated type stubs — regenerate them after upgrading either library:
make stubs

title: feat.talib
text:
feat.talib
TA-Lib indicators exposed with a pandas-friendly interface.
Every TA-Lib function is available under its usual name and accepts an OHLCV DataFrame with lowercase columns (open, high, low, close, volume) – the frame layout returned by qrt.vendors. Indicators that only need a price series also accept a plain Series (treated as close). The datetime index is preserved on the output.





q.feat.talib.RSI(ohlc) # Series named ‘rsi’ q.feat.talib.ATR(ohlc, timeperiod=20) # uses high/low/close q.feat.talib.MACD(ohlc) # DataFrame: macd, macdsignal, macdhist q.feat.talib.SMA(prices, timeperiod=20) # Series in, Series out



Use dir(q.feat.talib) to list all indicators, or help(q.feat.talib.RSI) for a function’s inputs and parameters.

title: feat.talib
text:
q.feat.talib.RSI(ohlc) # Series named ‘rsi’ q.feat.talib.ATR(ohlc, timeperiod=20) # uses high/low/close q.feat.talib.MACD(ohlc) # DataFrame: macd, macdsignal, macdhist q.feat.talib.SMA(prices, timeperiod=20) # Series in, Series out



Use dir(q.feat.talib) to list all indicators, or help(q.feat.talib.RSI) for a function’s inputs and parameters.

title: plot
text:
plot
plot
Opinionated static and interactive plots for quantitative research.
The first helpers focus on return streams: quick column charts, equity curves, drawdowns, and compact performance tearsheets. Use :mod:qrt.plot.interactive for Plotly figures with hover, zoom, and range selection.




 Back to top

title: utils
text:
utils
General-purpose helper functions.





Name
Description




log
Return the element-wise natural logarithm of scalar or array-like values.


set_seed
Set random seed for reproducibility across various libraries. If none is given, default is 42.





utils.log(values)
Return the element-wise natural logarithm of scalar or array-like values.
This delegates to :func:numpy.log, preserving pandas Series and DataFrame labels. For simple returns r, use log(1 + r) to obtain log returns.



utils.set_seed(seed=42, strict_determinism=False)
Set random seed for reproducibility across various libraries. If none is given, default is 42.











Name
Type
Description
Default




seed
int
Random seed to use.
42


strict_determinism
bool
If True, also forces deterministic cuDNN kernels, disables cuDNN auto-tuning, and enables torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True) for stronger reproducibility guarantees, at the cost of speed and possible errors (see Caveats). Defaults to False.
False







Enabling torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True) can raise a RuntimeError if an op has no deterministic implementation. The CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG environment variable is set to :4096:8 automatically (unless already set) as required by CUDA.
torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = True and torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = False can noticeably slow down training since cuDNN can no longer auto-tune kernels.
Determinism is not guaranteed across different PyTorch versions, hardware, or number of threads/devices.

bool
If True, also forces deterministic cuDNN kernels, disables cuDNN auto-tuning, and enables torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True) for stronger reproducibility guarantees, at the cost of speed and possible errors (see Caveats). Defaults to False.
False







Enabling torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True) can raise a RuntimeError if an op has no deterministic implementation. The CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG environment variable is set to :4096:8 automatically (unless already set) as required by CUDA.
torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = True and torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = False can noticeably slow down training since cuDNN can no longer auto-tune kernels.
Determinism is not guaranteed across different PyTorch versions, hardware, or number of threads/devices.

title: feat
text:
feat
feat
Feature engineering: indicators and wrappers around feature libraries.
Structured as a namespace of feature submodules:
q.feat.qta        -- our own hand-rolled features
q.feat.talib      -- every TA-Lib indicator, pandas-friendly
q.feat.pandas_ta  -- every pandas-ta-classic indicator
To add your own feature family, create qrt/feat/<name>.py with functions that take a Series/DataFrame and return one, then register it in the imports and __all__ below. It becomes available as q.feat.<name>.*.




 Back to top

title: vendors.yfinance
text:
vendors.yfinance
Yahoo Finance market data vendor (stocks, ETFs, indices).





Name
Description




YFinanceVendor
Vendor backed by the yfinance package.





vendors.yfinance.YFinanceVendor(cache_dir=DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR)
Vendor backed by the yfinance package.
Fetched OHLC data is cached locally as parquet files named like {symbol}-{interval}-{start}-{end}.parquet so repeated requests for the same range don’t hit the network.











Name
Type
Description
Default




cache_dir
str | Path
Directory for parquet OHLC caches.
DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR









Name
Description




fetch_ohlc
Fetch OHLCV bars from Yahoo Finance (cached as parquet).





vendors.yfinance.YFinanceVendor.fetch_ohlc(
    symbol,
    start_date,
    end_date,
    time_interval='1D',
)
Fetch OHLCV bars from Yahoo Finance (cached as parquet).
See :meth:DataVendor.fetch_ohlc. Only intervals supported by Yahoo are available (e.g. 1m, 5m, 1h, 1D); intraday data is limited to recent history by Yahoo.

title: Getting Started
text: QRT is an umbrella library of Quantitative Research Tools

title: Getting Started
text:
Installation
We use uv for environment and dependency management.
# create the environment and install qrt
uv sync

# run anything inside the environment
uv run python -c "import qrt as q"






Note



Not yet published to PyPI — install from source for now.


To use qrt from another uv project:
dependencies = ["qrt"]

[tool.uv.sources]
qrt = { git = "https://github.com/quantbert/qrt" }

title: Getting Started
text:
Quickstart
import qrt as q

# fetch OHLCV data (cached as parquet)
vendor = q.vendors.YFinance()
ohlc = vendor.fetch_ohlc("SPY", "2024-01-01", "2025-01-01", "1d")

# features
X = q.feat.qta.lags(ohlc["close"], [1, 5, 21])
X["sma_20"] = q.feat.qta.sma(ohlc["close"], 20)
X["rsi_14"] = q.feat.talib.RSI(ohlc)

title: Getting Started
text:
Library layout







Module
Purpose




q.feat
feature engineering: own indicators + TA-Lib + pandas-ta-classic


q.vendors
market data vendors (Yahoo Finance, Binance) with parquet caching


q.dataload
loading and aggregating raw trade data into OHLC bars


q.data
master securities database (DuckDB)


q.splits
leakage-aware CV splits (walk-forward, purged K-fold, embargo)


q.models
PyTorch model wrappers


q.bt
event-driven backtesting


q.plot
plotting + performance reports (tearsheets)


q.portfolio
portfolio analysis

title: Getting Started
text:
Development
make install   # sync the environment
make test      # run the test suite
make stubs     # regenerate .pyi stubs for the dynamic feat wrappers
make docs      # serve these docs locally

title: feat.pandas_ta
text:
feat.pandas_ta
pandas-ta-classic indicators exposed under the qrt feature namespace.
Every pandas-ta-classic indicator is available under its usual (lowercase) name and accepts an OHLCV DataFrame with lowercase columns (open, high, low, close, volume) – the frame layout returned by qrt.vendors. Indicators that only need a price series also accept a plain Series (treated as close). The datetime index is preserved and outputs keep pandas-ta’s parameterised names (e.g. RSI_14, MACD_12_26_9).





q.feat.pandas_ta.rsi(ohlc) # Series ‘RSI_14’ q.feat.pandas_ta.bbands(ohlc, length=20) # DataFrame BBL/BBM/BBU… q.feat.pandas_ta.cdl_pattern(ohlc, name=“doji”) q.feat.pandas_ta.rsi(prices) # Series in, Series out



Use dir(q.feat.pandas_ta) to list all indicators, or help(q.feat.pandas_ta.rsi) for a function’s parameters.

title: feat.pandas_ta
text:
q.feat.pandas_ta.rsi(ohlc) # Series ‘RSI_14’ q.feat.pandas_ta.bbands(ohlc, length=20) # DataFrame BBL/BBM/BBU… q.feat.pandas_ta.cdl_pattern(ohlc, name=“doji”) q.feat.pandas_ta.rsi(prices) # Series in, Series out



Use dir(q.feat.pandas_ta) to list all indicators, or help(q.feat.pandas_ta.rsi) for a function’s parameters.

title: vendors.base
text:
vendors.base
Common interface for market data vendors.





Name
Description




DataVendor
Abstract base class all data vendors implement.





vendors.base.DataVendor(cache_dir=DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR)
Abstract base class all data vendors implement.
A vendor knows how to fetch raw market data for a symbol and return it as a pandas DataFrame indexed by datetime. Concrete vendors may additionally expose vendor-specific methods (e.g. tick-level trades), but the methods below form the common contract.











Name
Type
Description
Default




cache_dir
str | Path
Directory for parquet caches of fetched data.
DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR









Name
Description




fetch_ohlc
Fetch OHLC(V) bars for symbol between two dates (inclusive).


fetch_ohlc_many
Fetch OHLCV bars for multiple symbols with a progress bar.





vendors.base.DataVendor.fetch_ohlc(
    symbol,
    start_date,
    end_date,
    time_interval='1h',
)
Fetch OHLC(V) bars for symbol between two dates (inclusive).











Name
Type
Description
Default




symbol
str
Vendor-specific symbol (e.g. "BTCUSDT", "AAPL").
required


start_date
str | datetime
Start date (inclusive), YYYY-MM-DD or datetime.
required


end_date
str | datetime
End date (inclusive), YYYY-MM-DD or datetime.
required


time_interval
str
Pandas offset alias (e.g. "1h", "5min", "1D").
'1h'














Name
Type
Description





pd.DataFrame
DataFrame indexed by datetime with columns



pd.DataFrame
open, high, low, close (and volume if available).







vendors.base.DataVendor.fetch_ohlc_many(
    symbols,
    start_date,
    end_date,
    time_interval='1D',
)
Fetch OHLCV bars for multiple symbols with a progress bar.
Symbols that fail are skipped with a warning.










Name
Type
Description





dict[str, pd.DataFrame]
Mapping of symbol to OHLCV DataFrame.













Name
Description




trades_to_ohlc
Aggregate tick trades into OHLCV bars.





vendors.base.trades_to_ohlc(trades, time_interval)
Aggregate tick trades into OHLCV bars.
Expects datetime, price and qty columns.

title: plot.interactive
text:
plot.interactive
Interactive Plotly charts for quantitative research return streams.





Name
Description




drawdown
Create an interactive underwater chart from periodic returns.


equity
Create an interactive compounded equity curve from periodic returns.


line
Create an interactive line chart from selected Series or DataFrame columns.


monthly_heatmap
Create an interactive, annotated calendar-month return heatmap.


performance
Create an interactive equity, drawdown, and headline-statistics report.


show
Display a figure, optionally saving it to save_to as PNG (default) and/or self-contained HTML.





plot.interactive.drawdown(
    returns,
    *,
    return_type='simple',
    title='Drawdown',
    height=320,
)
Create an interactive underwater chart from periodic returns.



plot.interactive.equity(
    returns,
    *,
    return_type='simple',
    title='Equity curve',
    label=None,
    height=450,
)
Create an interactive compounded equity curve from periodic returns.



plot.interactive.line(
    data,
    columns=None,
    *,
    title=None,
    yaxis_title=None,
    height=450,
)
Create an interactive line chart from selected Series or DataFrame columns.
Column selection supports the same shell-style patterns as :func:qrt.plot.col. The returned Plotly figure supports hover, zoom, pan, and range selection.



plot.interactive.monthly_heatmap(
    returns,
    *,
    return_type='simple',
    title='Monthly returns',
    height=None,
)
Create an interactive, annotated calendar-month return heatmap.



plot.interactive.performance(
    returns,
    *,
    benchmark=None,
    return_type='simple',
    periods_per_year=None,
    title=None,
    height=700,
)
Create an interactive equity, drawdown, and headline-statistics report.
The figure has linked time axes, unified hover, zooming, and date-range buttons. If supplied, the benchmark is aligned to the strategy’s shared observations before plotting.



plot.interactive.show(
    figure,
    name=None,
    *,
    save_to=None,
    formats=('png',),
    width=1400,
    height=800,
    scale=2,
)
Display a figure, optionally saving it to save_to as PNG (default) and/or self-contained HTML.

title: feat.qta
text:
feat.qta
qta: our own hand-rolled features, one module per feature.



Create qrt/feat/qta/<feature>.py (lowercase file name).
Define one public function taking a Series/DataFrame and returning one, with the index preserved. Helpers should be prefixed with _.
Re-export it below (one import line + one __all__ entry), so it is visible to IDEs and available as q.feat.qta.<feature>.

title: feat.qta
text:
Create qrt/feat/qta/<feature>.py (lowercase file name).
Define one public function taking a Series/DataFrame and returning one, with the index preserved. Helpers should be prefixed with _.
Re-export it below (one import line + one __all__ entry), so it is visible to IDEs and available as q.feat.qta.<feature>.

title: dataload
text:
dataload
Data loading from local file caches.





Name
Description




load_ohlc_timeseries_range
Load daily trade CSV files and aggregate them into OHLC bars.





dataload.load_ohlc_timeseries_range(
    sym,
    time_interval,
    start_date,
    end_date,
    data_path='./cache',
)
Load daily trade CSV files and aggregate them into OHLC bars.
Expects daily files named like {sym}-trades-YYYY-MM-DD.csv, e.g. BTCUSDT-trades-2025-09-22.csv, containing at least a datetime and a price column. A qty column, if present, is summed into a volume column.











Name
Type
Description
Default




sym
str
Symbol prefix (e.g. "BTCUSDT").
required


time_interval
str
Pandas offset alias (e.g. "1h", "5min").
required


start_date
datetime
Start datetime (inclusive).
required


end_date
datetime
End datetime (inclusive).
required


data_path
str | Path
Directory containing the cached trade CSV files.
'./cache'














Name
Type
Description





pd.DataFrame
DataFrame indexed by datetime with columns



pd.DataFrame
open, high, low, close (and volume if available).

title: vendors
text:
vendors
Market data vendors.
Each vendor implements the :class:~qrt.vendors.base.DataVendor interface, so downstream code can stay vendor-agnostic:
import qrt as q

vendor = q.vendors.get_vendor("binance")
ohlc = vendor.fetch_ohlc("BTCUSDT", "2025-01-01", "2025-01-07", "1h")
To add a new vendor, subclass DataVendor in a new module and register it in _VENDORS below.





Name
Description




get_vendor
Instantiate a vendor by name.





vendors.get_vendor(name, **kwargs)
Instantiate a vendor by name.











Name
Type
Description
Default




name
str

title: vendors.binance
text:
vendors.binance
Binance futures market data vendor.
Downloads daily tick-level trade dumps from data.binance.vision and caches them locally as parquet. OHLC bars are aggregated from trades.





Name
Description




BinanceVendor
Vendor for Binance USD-M futures daily trade dumps.





vendors.binance.BinanceVendor(download_dir='data', cache_dir=DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR)
Vendor for Binance USD-M futures daily trade dumps.











Name
Type
Description
Default




download_dir
str | Path
Directory for temporary zip/csv downloads.
'data'


cache_dir
str | Path
Directory for parquet trade caches.
DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR









Name
Description




fetch_ohlc
Fetch OHLCV bars aggregated from tick trades.


fetch_trades
Fetch trades for a date range (inclusive), concatenated.


fetch_trades_day
Fetch all trades for symbol on a single day (cached as parquet).





vendors.binance.BinanceVendor.fetch_ohlc(
    symbol,
    start_date,
    end_date,
    time_interval='1h',
)
Fetch OHLCV bars aggregated from tick trades.
See :meth:DataVendor.fetch_ohlc. Bars are aggregated per day and concatenated, so intervals should divide evenly into one day.



vendors.binance.BinanceVendor.fetch_trades(symbol, start_date, end_date)
Fetch trades for a date range (inclusive), concatenated.
Days that fail to download are skipped with a warning.



vendors.binance.BinanceVendor.fetch_trades_day(symbol, date)
Fetch all trades for symbol on a single day (cached as parquet).











Name
Type
Description
Default




symbol
str
Binance symbol, e.g. "BTCUSDT".
required


date
str | datetime
Day to fetch, YYYY-MM-DD or datetime.
required














Name
Type
Description





pd.DataFrame
DataFrame with columns id, price, qty, quote_qty, time, | |        | pd.DataFrame | is_buyer_maker, datetime.
